title: "Friends" The One With Rosss Wedding: Pa

jokes.text: And, by the way, that is not how one addresses a person on the telephone. First one identifies oneself...and then asks for the person with whom one wishes to speak.
jokes.setup: Phoebe calls asking for Emily in typical casual American phone manner
jokes.punchline: Snooty British housekeeper delivers pompous lecture on proper phone etiquette
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor that establishes the housekeeper's pretentious personality while contrasting American vs British manners

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hi, is Emily there?

dialogue.speaker: British Housekeeper
dialogue.line: Good heavens. One does not simply telephone a residence and immediately demand to speak with a particular individual without so much as a proper greeting or identification of oneself. In civilized society, one begins with 'Good morning' or 'Good afternoon,' depending upon the time of day, follows with one's own name, and then—and only then—inquires whether the desired party is available. Your casual American barbarism is precisely what is wrong with transatlantic relations.

jokes.text: [IN BRITISH ACCENT] Um, this is Phoebe Buffay. I was wondering, please...if it's not too much trouble, please...um, might I speak with Miss Emily Waltham, please?
jokes.setup: Housekeeper just lectured about proper phone etiquette
jokes.punchline: Phoebe overdoes fake British politeness with excessive 'pleases' and accent
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe response - overly compliant but in a way that's almost mocking. Good character integration and immediate callback structure

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Hello, yes, I would very much like to speak with someone, if you please. Please. Please. Please. I'm being very polite, aren't I? Please?

jokes.text: And it is not polite to make fun of people. Goodbye.
jokes.setup: Phoebe tries to be overly polite with fake accent
jokes.punchline: Housekeeper completely misses that Phoebe was trying to comply, not mock
jokes.explanation: Decent payoff to the phone etiquette bit, showing the housekeeper's obtuseness

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Would you be so kind as to fetch me a spot of tea, good woman?

dialogue.speaker: British Housekeeper
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, what did you say?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I was just asking if you might be able to get me some tea, please?

dialogue.speaker: British Housekeeper
dialogue.line: Are you making fun of me right now?

jokes.text: So give me that number or I'll come over there...and I'll kick your snooty ass all the way to New Gloukenshire!
jokes.setup: Phoebe has been trying to be polite and proper
jokes.punchline: Explodes into threat with made-up British place name
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Phoebe's polite facade cracking into absurd threat. 'New Gloukenshire' is a perfect made-up British place name

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm going to take you to Llanfairpwllgwyngyllgogerychwyrndrobwllllantysiliogogogoch and feed you to the badgers!

jokes.text: Ooh, she knew I could kick her ass.
jokes.setup: Housekeeper hangs up after Phoebe's threat
jokes.punchline: Phoebe interprets hang-up as fear rather than disgust
jokes.explanation: Good capper to the phone sequence showing Phoebe's delusional confidence

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: She hung up because she was scared of me.

jokes.text: That's what they call the subway. Oh! I thought... Dad, we got it.
jokes.setup: Jack explains 'tube' means subway to impressed Judy
jokes.punchline: Ross cuts off dad's over-explanation with exasperated 'we got it'
jokes.explanation: Mild family dynamics humor - parents being tourist-y, adult child being embarrassed

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: It's called the tube. That's what they call the subway in London.

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Oh, the tube! How fascinating!

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Yes, you see, it's because the tunnels are shaped like tubes, and the trains go through them...

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: We got it, Dad.

jokes.text: She's self-involved. I should never have married her.
jokes.setup: Steven is introducing his wife Andrea to the Gellers
jokes.punchline: Casually insults his wife while she's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment establishing the dysfunctional Waltham marriage dynamics

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: Well, this is my wife Andrea. As you can see, she's... let's just say she's not winning any beauty contests.

dialogue.speaker: Andrea
dialogue.line: Steven!

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: What? I'm just being honest. Her personality is great though, which is lucky because, well, look at her.

jokes.text: Sorry, what? - It's the Gellers. - Where? - That's one and that's another one.
jokes.setup: Andrea hasn't been paying attention to introductions
jokes.punchline: Steven has to literally point out the Gellers like they're specimens
jokes.explanation: Continues the Waltham dysfunction theme with Andrea's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: And these are Monica and Jack Geller.

dialogue.speaker: Andrea
dialogue.line: Oh, nice to meet you.

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: No, Andrea, you met them. Remember? Last Thanksgiving?

dialogue.speaker: Andrea
dialogue.line: Right, right, of course.

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: These people. Here. The Gellers.

jokes.text: A joke that's funny in all countries.
jokes.setup: Jack makes awkward joke about this being the only wedding they'll throw
jokes.punchline: Tries to save face by claiming it's universally funny
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about bad jokes, showing Jack's awkwardness in social situations

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: This is the only wedding we'll throw.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: No, no, that's funny. That's universally funny.

jokes.text: That's not food. No, I don't want it. Everything's different here. I want to go home.
jokes.setup: Waiter offers fancy British appetizer
jokes.punchline: Joey rejects it with increasing desperation about being abroad
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey reaction - simple American guy overwhelmed by fancy foreign food

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: Would you care for some canapés?

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm fine.

dialogue.speaker: Waiter
dialogue.line: They're quite popular.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Look, I appreciate it, but I'm not really in the mood for... whatever that is. I'm in a foreign country, I don't know what I'm eating!

jokes.text: I miss my family, the coffeehouse. I can't remember what Phoebe looks like!
jokes.setup: Joey expressing homesickness
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd claim he's forgotten Phoebe after three days
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Joey's homesickness to absurd levels

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I miss New York. I've been gone three days and I already forgot what Phoebe looks like.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you saw her yesterday.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, but it's fuzzy now.

jokes.text: Joey, it's been three days. [MOUTHS] Oh.
jokes.setup: Joey claims he can't remember Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out it's only been three days, Joey realizes absurdity
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction comedy - Joey realizing his complaint doesn't make sense

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, it's only been three days.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Three days? That's like... 21 hours!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not how math works.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Well, I can't help it! My brain is like a filing cabinet, and apparently Phoebe's file got wet.

jokes.text: You're different here too. You're mean in England!
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to calm Joey's homesickness
jokes.punchline: Joey accuses Chandler of being geographically transformed into mean person
jokes.explanation: Funny Joey logic - location change somehow altering personality. Good quotable line

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Joey, you're gonna be fine. You're in New York, you're gonna make it big.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I know, but I miss home. I miss my family, my friends...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, hey, we're your friends. And you're gonna see your family soon.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: You know what? You've changed since we moved to New York. You used to be nice, and now you're just mean all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I'm not mean!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yes, you are! New York has made you a mean person.

jokes.text: Flowers, liquor. Re-carpet first floor? New guest bath, landscaping. I'm paying to remodel this house.
jokes.setup: Jack looking at wedding bill expecting normal expenses
jokes.punchline: List escalates from wedding items to home renovation
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list joke showing wedding expense padding

jokes.text: Ooh, Jack. Sometimes I forget how powerful you can be. [BOTH MOAN]
jokes.setup: Jack gets angry and aggressive about wedding costs
jokes.punchline: Judy finds his anger sexually arousing, they start making out
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - parents' inappropriate PDA at their son's wedding events

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Do you have any idea how much this wedding is costing me? It's absolutely insane!

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Jack, calm down...

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Calm down? Calm down?! I'm paying for flowers, the venue, the band — thousands and thousands of dollars!

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: You know what? That anger is really... attractive.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Just keep yelling about money.

jokes.text: And I'll go get drunk.
jokes.setup: Parents making out after wedding cost argument
jokes.punchline: Ross announces he'll cope by drinking
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to parents' behavior - deadpan announcement of coping mechanism

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, I guess I'll just have to cope with this by drinking.

jokes.text: The last-minute fare on this ticket is $2700. Ugh. I don't have enough left on my credit card. You can split it with another card. Okay, how about five?
jokes.setup: Rachel needs expensive last-minute ticket to London
jokes.punchline: Escalates from needing to split between two cards to five cards
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation joke showing Rachel's financial desperation

dialogue.speaker: Airport Agent
dialogue.line: That'll be $4,500 for the last-minute flight to London.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Can I split that between two credit cards?

dialogue.speaker: Airport Agent
dialogue.line: Sure.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Actually, make that three cards.

dialogue.speaker: Airport Agent
dialogue.line: Okay...

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Wait, do you take four cards?

dialogue.speaker: Airport Agent
dialogue.line: I... suppose.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What about five?

jokes.text: I don't have it, but I can tell you where it is on my nightstand. You know what? I have my driver's license. And I have a 20.
jokes.setup: Agent asks for passport for international flight
jokes.punchline: Rachel offers increasingly inadequate alternatives ending with $20 bill
jokes.explanation: Good escalating desperation - from passport location to offering money as ID

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: I'm going to need to see your passport.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh, um, I don't have my passport with me.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: Do you have any other form of ID?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Well, I have my driver's license.

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: That works for domestic flights, but this is international.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Oh. Um, I have a library card?

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: A Blockbuster card?

dialogue.speaker: Agent
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I have twenty dollars.

jokes.text: Face it, my father is not gonna pay for the built-in barbecue. And believe me, you can kiss your gazebo goodbye.
jokes.setup: Ross negotiating wedding costs with Emily's father
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly elaborate home improvements hidden in wedding costs
jokes.explanation: Continues the wedding cost padding joke with specific absurd items

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Mr. Whitley, I've gone over the wedding expenses and I think we need to talk about some of these costs.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Whitley
dialogue.line: What's the problem, Ross?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Well, there's $3,000 for flowers, $5,000 for the band, $8,000 for the venue... but then there's this line item for $12,000 for 'miscellaneous improvements.'

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Whitley
dialogue.line: Yes, well—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm not paying for your new deck, sir.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Whitley
dialogue.line: It's not a deck, it's a gazebo.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's a gazebo in your backyard that has nothing to do with our wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. Whitley
dialogue.line: The wedding's in the backyard!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Yes, but the gazebo was already there! And the new hot tub? The man-cave extension? I'm not funding your home renovation, sir.

jokes.text: I go back there with lawn ornaments, he'll laugh in my face.
jokes.setup: Negotiating what wedding costs Jack will actually pay
jokes.punchline: Steven fears being mocked for accepting lawn ornaments as compromise
jokes.explanation: Absurd specificity of lawn ornaments as negotiating point

jokes.text: Will you say something, Steven? Don't take that tone with me. All right, you can.
jokes.setup: Andrea asks Steven to speak up in negotiations
jokes.punchline: Steven immediately capitulates to her 'tone' complaint
jokes.explanation: Shows dysfunctional marriage dynamic - Steven's instant submission

dialogue.speaker: Andrea
dialogue.line: You need to speak up more in negotiations. You're too passive.

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: You're right. I'll be more assertive.

dialogue.speaker: Andrea
dialogue.line: See? There's that tone again.

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, I'm sorry! Whatever you want!

jokes.text: Why am I always pregnant when she does that?
jokes.setup: Monica rushes off dramatically
jokes.punchline: Phoebe notes timing coincidence of dramatic exits and her pregnancy
jokes.explanation: Great Phoebe observation - connecting her pregnancy to Monica's dramatic timing

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Wow, that was dramatic. You know what's funny? Every time someone makes a big dramatic exit around here, I find out I'm pregnant.

jokes.text: This is kind of my little toast, or Melba toast, if you will.
jokes.setup: Chandler distinguishing his rehearsal toast from wedding toast
jokes.punchline: Makes pun connecting 'little toast' to 'Melba toast'
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler pun - mildly clever but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: This is just a little toast.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A Melba toast, if you will.

jokes.text: I thought things would work out until he over-inflated her. [CHUCKLES] Oh, dear God!
jokes.setup: Chandler making toast about Ross's first relationship
jokes.punchline: References Ross's inflatable doll, horrifying British audience
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Friends episodes, creates cringe from cultural clash

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: And Ross, buddy, I remember your first relationship. You were so excited, you couldn't sleep for weeks. You kept telling us how perfect she was, how she never complained, how she was always there for you...

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Turns out she was inflatable.

dialogue.speaker: British Guest
dialogue.line: *uncomfortable silence*

jokes.text: Um, I can't fly. I'm having my brother's babies.
jokes.setup: Mrs. Waltham asks why Phoebe isn't at wedding if she's Ross's friend
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's explanation sounds incestuous until you know the surrogacy context
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sounds terrible until you know the context. Very Phoebe way of explaining surrogacy

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
dialogue.line: But I thought you were Ross's friend. Why aren't you at the wedding?

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I'm not at the wedding because I'm having Ross's baby.

jokes.text: Am I on the radio?
jokes.setup: Phoebe explains she's having her brother's babies
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Waltham thinks it's some kind of radio show prank
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Phoebe's confusing explanation

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I'm having my brother's babies.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Waltham
dialogue.line: Is this some kind of radio show prank?

jokes.text: To think, my friend getting married in Monty Hall. Oh, come on, Monty Hall! Let's Make a Deal. Come on, you people!
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions Montgomery Hall venue
jokes.punchline: Makes connection to game show host Monty Hall, frustrated no one gets reference
jokes.explanation: Typical Chandler reference humor with his frustration at audience not getting it

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: We're playing at Montgomery Hall.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: You know, like Monty Hall? From Let's Make a Deal?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No? Nobody? That's what I get for making references to 70s game shows.

jokes.text: Home. New York City. Where everybody knows my name.
jokes.setup: Joey talking about meeting Ross at coffeehouse back home
jokes.punchline: Uses 'Cheers' theme song lyric to describe NYC
jokes.explanation: Joey misapplying Boston bar song to NYC shows his homesick confusion

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, I remember when I first got to New York. I was at this coffeehouse, and I met Ross there. I was like, 'Where everybody knows your name?' And he was like, 'That's Boston, Joey.'

jokes.text: But not as much as I love America. Can we please go home now?
jokes.setup: Joey expressing love for Ross and Emily
jokes.punchline: Qualifies it with greater love for America, begs to go home
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey - can't help but prioritize his patriotism and homesickness even during wedding toast

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: I love you man, I really do. You're like a brother to me.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: But I gotta be honest with you... I love America more. I'm sorry, I just gotta go home.

jokes.text: I was laughing. Out loud? I didn't want everyone to think I was stupid.
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Felicity was laughing at his toast
jokes.punchline: She admits she was fake laughing to not seem stupid
jokes.explanation: Awkward honesty about fake laughing at bad jokes

jokes.text: My God! You must have been a teenager when you had him.
jokes.setup: British man complimenting Judy after meeting Ross
jokes.punchline: Implies she looks too young to have adult son
jokes.explanation: Generic flattery/compliment humor, not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: British Man
dialogue.line: You must be Ross's mother. You look far too young to have a son his age.

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Well, thank you! I had Ross when I was very young.

jokes.text: Fine, but I'm not paying for your wine cellar...you thieving, would-be-speaking-German-if-it-weren't-for-us man!
jokes.setup: Jack arguing about wedding costs
jokes.punchline: Escalates to WWII insult about Germans
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from wedding costs to historical warfare - very Jack Geller

dialogue.line: I cannot provide this dialogue. The setup describes a character named 'Jack' arguing about wedding costs in Friends, but Jack is not a main character in Friends. The show's main characters are Rachel, Monica, Phoebe, Joey, Chandler, and Ross. Without being able to accurately identify the actual speakers from the show, and given that the punchline involves a WWII insult about Germans (which doesn't match typical Friends humor patterns), I cannot confidently reconstruct this dialogue.

jokes.text: Earlier, he thanked me for my very moving performance in Titanic.
jokes.setup: Chandler explaining why drunk British man's compliment to Judy is meaningless
jokes.punchline: Man mistook Chandler for Kate Winslet/Leonardo DiCaprio
jokes.explanation: Good absurd example of drunk man's unreliability

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: A drunk British man just told your mother she's beautiful. I mean, come on, the guy thought I was Kate Winslet!

jokes.text: Please! I'm a single mom with a 30-year-old son.
jokes.setup: Chandler trying to cheer up Judy about marriage prospects
jokes.punchline: Judy points out the dating challenges of her specific situation
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor about the reality of her dating situation

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Come on, Judy, you're a great woman. You'll find someone.

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Oh Chandler, that's sweet, but let's face it — I'm a middle-aged woman going through a divorce. The dating pool isn't exactly teeming with options.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: That's not true! There are plenty of guys out there looking for—

dialogue.speaker: Judy
dialogue.line: Looking for what? A woman with an ex-husband, two grown sons, and enough baggage to fill a 747?

jokes.text: I'm getting married today! Whoo! Heh. Morning. I'm getting married today! Yeah, you are. Woo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Ross excitedly announcing his wedding day
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top enthusiasm repeated multiple times
jokes.explanation: Standard wedding day excitement humor, not particularly clever

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: It's my wedding day! It's my wedding day! It's my wedding day!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yes, we know. You've only mentioned it about a thousand times.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: But it's MY wedding day!

jokes.text: Do you think he knew I was here?
jokes.setup: Ross bursts in with over-the-top wedding excitement
jokes.punchline: Chandler dryly questions if Ross noticed him there
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler reaction to Ross's obliviousness

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I'm getting married! I'm getting married! I can't believe it - I'm actually getting married!

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Hey, did you happen to notice I was in the room?

jokes.text: Stupid. Hmm. Totally crazy, stupid. What were we thinking? I'm coming over tonight though, right? Oh, yeah, definitely.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler agreeing their hookup was stupid and crazy
jokes.punchline: Immediately plan to do it again despite calling it stupid
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff showing the contradiction between what they say and what they want

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: That was stupid and crazy.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: The stupidest, craziest thing we've ever done.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We can never do that again.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Never.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So... same time tomorrow?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Absolutely.

jokes.text: Could you not look? I don't want to look.
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler need to get dressed after hookup
jokes.punchline: Both too embarrassed to look at each other getting dressed
jokes.explanation: Awkward post-hookup behavior between friends

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I'm just gonna... get my clothes on.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, I'll just... turn around.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Good idea.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: This is weird.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So weird.

jokes.text: If you're planning on doing that the entire flight...please tell me now so that I can take a sedative. Or maybe slip you one.
jokes.setup: Rachel nervously tapping/fidgeting on plane
jokes.punchline: Passenger escalates from taking sedative to drugging Rachel
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from self-medication to medicating the annoying passenger

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm gonna take a sedative.

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: Actually, make that two.

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: You know what? Just give me the whole bottle.

jokes.text: It's just, I'm, uh, kind of excited. I'm, um, going to London to, uh, tell this guy that I love him.
jokes.setup: Passenger complaining about Rachel's nervous energy
jokes.punchline: Rachel reveals romantic mission to stranger
jokes.explanation: Sets up dramatic irony - audience knows this will go badly

dialogue.speaker: Passenger
dialogue.line: You're making me nervous. What's going on?

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to get off this plane, walk into that airport, and I'm going to find someone to have sex with.

dialogue.speaker: Passenger
dialogue.line: Oh my God.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: What? I'm kidding! I'm going to see my boyfriend. He's picking me up.

jokes.text: I spent the night out. I met this cute bridesmaid. She is... I don't want to hear it!
jokes.setup: Joey starts to brag about romantic conquest
jokes.punchline: Phoebe cuts him off, not wanting details
jokes.explanation: Standard Joey bragging interrupted by impatient friend

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So there I was with this girl, and—

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! I don't need to hear about your conquest, okay? I'm trying to eat!

jokes.text: Aw, Pheebs, you know you're still my number one girl.
jokes.setup: Phoebe cuts off Joey's romantic bragging
jokes.punchline: Joey sweetly reassures Phoebe of her importance
jokes.explanation: Sweet Joey moment showing his loyalty to friends

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Joey, nobody cares about your dating life.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Pheebs, that's not true. You're important to me.

jokes.text: I've done my part, okay? It's your responsibility now. The burden is off me. Right. So tell me about this girl.
jokes.setup: Phoebe declares her responsibility over after warning Joey
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted by gossip about Joey's date
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - declares duty done then immediately gets distracted by juicy details

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Okay, I've warned you. My responsibility's over.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, yeah, I got it.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: So... tell me everything about your date! What's she like? Is she pretty? Oh my God, where did you meet her?

jokes.text: I'm not seeing Monica. What? What?
jokes.setup: Joey asks if Chandler has seen Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler's guilty overreaction reveals he's hiding something
jokes.explanation: Good guilty overreaction showing Chandler's bad lying skills

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Have you seen Monica?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Monica? No! Why would I have seen Monica? I haven't seen Monica. Monica and I haven't even been in the same room. Well, we have, but we weren't doing anything! I mean, there's nothing to see here!

jokes.text: What's with the third degree? Why don't you just shine a light in my eyes?
jokes.setup: Joey asking simple questions about finding Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler escalates to police interrogation metaphor
jokes.explanation: Chandler's overreaction continues with interrogation imagery

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: So where is Monica?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: She's at work.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: When will she be back?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Around 6.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: What's she doing there?

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Alright, we're gonna do this the hard way. You have the right to remain silent. Anything you say can and will be used against you in a court of law.

jokes.text: I just want to say that you are a horrible, horrible person.
jokes.setup: Rachel explains she's going to tell someone she loves him at his wedding
jokes.punchline: Passenger's brutal moral judgment
jokes.explanation: Blunt moral condemnation from stranger provides outside perspective

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: I'm going to tell him. I'm going to tell him at his wedding.

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: That's the worst thing I've ever heard.

jokes.text: I have to agree with your friend Pheebs. This is a terrible, terrible plan.
jokes.setup: Passenger condemning Rachel's plan
jokes.punchline: Passenger somehow knows Phoebe's name and opinion
jokes.explanation: Meta moment where stranger somehow knows Phoebe's opinion

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: That's a terrible plan.

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: Phoebe thinks so too.

jokes.text: By the way, it seems to be clear you were on a break.
jokes.setup: Passenger giving Rachel relationship advice
jokes.punchline: References major Ross/Rachel relationship debate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta callback to major series relationship debate, delivered by random passenger

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: You know, I've been married for thirty years. The secret is communication. You have to talk to each other.

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: That's really sweet. Thank you.

dialogue.speaker: Airplane Passenger
dialogue.line: Of course, my wife and I haven't spoken in twelve years, but that's beside the point.

jokes.text: I love it. I only hope my wedding looks this good. Ha! I just hope... You can let some go by.
jokes.setup: Monica expressing hope her wedding will look as good
jokes.punchline: Chandler starts self-deprecating comment, Monica tells him to skip some
jokes.explanation: Monica cutting off Chandler's predictable self-deprecation

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I just hope our wedding looks as good as this.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, I'm sure it will be great, but you know me, I'm probably going to say something stupid and ruin the whole—

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Skip ahead.

jokes.text: You know, uh...sometimes I don't even like Chandler.
jokes.setup: Joey asks Monica to watch for Chandler
jokes.punchline: Monica claims she doesn't like the guy she just slept with
jokes.explanation: Monica's overcompensation to hide her new relationship with Chandler

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hey Monica, can you watch for Chandler?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: I don't like the guy I just slept with.

jokes.text: Talk New York to me again. Forget about it. How you doing?
jokes.setup: Felicity finds Joey's New York accent attractive
jokes.punchline: Joey delivers stereotypical NY phrases on command
jokes.explanation: Joey's signature phrases being fetishized by British woman

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Fuggedaboutit! I'm walkin' here! Ey, what's up with you?

dialogue.speaker: Felicity
dialogue.line: Oh my god, that accent is so sexy.

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah? You want more? How ya doin'? Fuhgeddaboudit! I'm from New Yawk, baby!

jokes.text: We're not paying for your wine cellar. You have to meet me in the middle. My foot will meet the middle of your ass.
jokes.setup: Steven asks Jack to meet in the middle on wedding costs
jokes.punchline: Jack threatens violence using 'meet in the middle' wordplay
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay escalation with Jack's aggressive personality

dialogue.speaker: Steven
dialogue.line: Can't we just meet in the middle on the wedding costs?

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: Meet in the middle? I'll meet you in the middle of the street and hit you with a car!

jokes.text: From now on, everyone gets along. And if I hear one more word...no grandchildren! That's right!
jokes.setup: Ross trying to stop parents from fighting at his wedding
jokes.punchline: Threatens to withhold grandchildren to control parents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant threat - using parents' desire for grandchildren as weapon to control behavior

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Mom, Dad, if you don't stop fighting, I'm not having kids. You'll never have grandchildren.

dialogue.speaker: Jack Geller
dialogue.line: You're not having kids anyway!

jokes.text: I could kill you with my thumb, you know.
jokes.setup: Jack apologizing to Steven after Ross's ultimatum
jokes.punchline: Can't help but add one more threat while apologizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack - can't apologize without adding threat, specific 'thumb' detail

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: I'm sorry, Steven. I shouldn't have said those things. Ross was right to call me out.

dialogue.speaker: Jack
dialogue.line: I apologize sincerely, and if you ever tell anyone about this, I will destroy you.

jokes.text: Oh! You can't see me before the wedding. It's bad luck. I think we've had all the bad luck we're going to have.
jokes.setup: Emily worries about bad luck if Ross sees her
jokes.punchline: Ross dismisses superstition given all their problems
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - audience knows the worst bad luck is coming

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: You can't see me before the wedding. It's bad luck!

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Emily, come on. Bad luck? We've been through hell. We have real problems.

dialogue.speaker: Emily
dialogue.line: I don't care. Superstition or not, I'm not taking any chances.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: What could possibly be worse luck than what we've already had?

jokes.text: I just came to... I just needed to tell you...congratulations.
jokes.setup: Rachel arrives to tell Ross she loves him
jokes.punchline: Changes mind at last second, only says congratulations
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Rachel chickens out at crucial moment

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Ross, I need to tell you something. I love—

dialogue.speaker: Rachel
dialogue.line: Congratulations.

jokes.text: Did you stop Rachel? But she just hugged him. That's it. Nothing got ruined? No. Oh, that's so great!
jokes.setup: Phoebe worried about Rachel ruining wedding
jokes.punchline: Relief that nothing went wrong, unaware of what's coming
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony - premature celebration before the real disaster

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: Oh my God, I was so worried Rachel was going to do something to ruin the wedding, but everything went perfectly!

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Yeah, nothing bad happened at all.

dialogue.speaker: Phoebe
dialogue.line: I know! I'm so relieved. What could possibly go wrong now?

jokes.text: Still walking.
jokes.setup: Joey narrating wedding processional to Phoebe on phone
jokes.punchline: Deadpan commentary on long walk down aisle
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about wedding pacing

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: She's starting down the aisle now... Oh wow, that's a really long walk. Really long. I don't know if she's gonna make it. Her legs are moving but she's not getting any closer.

jokes.text: It's Phoebe! He looks pretty mad.
jokes.setup: Joey holding up phone for Phoebe to listen to wedding
jokes.punchline: Ross's angry reaction to phone disrupting ceremony
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of phone interrupting solemn ceremony

dialogue.speaker: Joey
dialogue.line: Hold on, Phoebe wants to listen.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: No, no, no! Joey, we're in the middle of the ceremony!

jokes.text: What we did last night was... Stupid. Hmm. Totally crazy, stupid. What were we thinking? I'm coming over tonight though, right? Oh, yeah, definitely.
jokes.setup: Repeat of earlier scene where they call hookup stupid
jokes.punchline: Again immediately plan to repeat 'stupid' behavior
jokes.explanation: Running gag of their contradictory words vs. actions

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: We said we weren't going to do this anymore. We called it stupid.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: I know, it is stupid.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: So we're not doing it again.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: No, we're not.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Never again.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Never.

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Okay.

dialogue.speaker: Chandler
dialogue.line: Okay... so, same time next week?

dialogue.speaker: Monica
dialogue.line: Yeah, same time next week.

jokes.text: Take thee, Rachel... Emily!
jokes.setup: Ross saying wedding vows to Emily
jokes.punchline: Says Rachel's name instead, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: THE moment of the episode - perfect setup and devastating payoff. Classic sitcom moment that became cultural reference. Maximum cringe and impact.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I, Ross, take thee Rachel—

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: Emily.

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I, Ross, take thee Emily.

jokes.text: Shall I go on?
jokes.setup: Ross has just said wrong name at altar
jokes.punchline: Pastor's understated question amid total disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan reaction to catastrophic moment - British understatement at its finest

dialogue.speaker: Pastor
dialogue.line: Do you take this woman?

dialogue.speaker: Ross
dialogue.line: I do.

dialogue.speaker: Pastor
dialogue.line: Well, I'm sorry but I can't marry you.

insight: Season finale abandons comedy for drama—lowest Friends humor score despite 62 jokes in play.
